challenges.title: Char Arrays and String Class
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that declares a char array (C-string) initialized with a name and a `string` class variable initialized with a greeting. Concatenate them together into a new `string` variable and print the result. Also print the length of the final string using `.length()` or `.size()`. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'char ' in cpp and '[' in cpp, 'Declare a char array (C-string) for the name'
assert 'string ' in cpp, 'Use the string class for at least one variable'
assert '.length()' in cpp or '.size()' in cpp, 'Use .length() or .size() to get the string length'
assert '+' in cpp or 'append' in cpp or 'strcat' in cpp, 'Concatenate the strings together using + or append'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Print the result using cout'
hints: Declare a C-string with: char name[] = "Alice";
The string class supports + for concatenation: string result = greeting + name;

challenges.title: C-String Functions and String Methods
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that demonstrates both C-string functions and string class methods. Use `strlen` to get the length of a char array, `strcpy` to copy one C-string to another, and use the string class methods `.find()` and `.substr()` on a string object. Print all results. Include `<cstring>` for the C-string functions. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'strlen' in cpp, 'Use strlen() to get the length of a C-string'
assert 'strcpy' in cpp, 'Use strcpy() to copy one C-string to another'
assert '.find(' in cpp, 'Use the string class .find() method'
assert '.substr(' in cpp, 'Use the string class .substr() method'
assert 'cstring' in cpp or 'string.h' in cpp, 'Include <cstring> or <string.h> for C-string functions'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Print the results using cout'
hints: strlen(str) returns the length; strcpy(dest, src) copies src into dest
string::find() returns the position of a substring; substr(pos, len) extracts a portion
